steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: After acknowledging a geographic shortcoming in their research, the researchers gained new banking records from Africa and Eastern Europe. The blank describes what they do to that previous limitation with their expanded data. The word must mean something like 'remedy' or 'improve.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'sanction': Means to officially permit or penalize. New data does not permit or penalize a research gap. ✗
  • Choice B — 'postulate': Means to propose or assume. Researchers are not assuming the limitation exists — they already acknowledged it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'ameliorate': Means to improve or make something bad less severe. New data from under-researched regions directly improves the geographic shortcoming the researchers acknowledged. ✓
  • Choice D — 'rationalize': Means to justify or explain away. The researchers are not justifying the gap — they are fixing it with new data. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
